title.en: SWE-Explore: Benchmarking How Coding Agents Explore Repositories
title.zh: SWE-Explore：基准测试编码代理如何探索代码仓库

abstract: Repository-level coding benchmarks such as SWE-bench have driven a rapid surge in the capabilities of coding agents. Yet they usually treat coding tasks as a holistic, binary prediction problem (e.g., resolved or unresolved), neglecting fine-grained agent capabilities such as repository understanding, context retrieval, code localization, and bug diagnosis. In this paper, we introduce SWE-Explore, a benchmark that isolates the evaluation of repository exploration, a critical capability of coding agents. Given a repository and an issue, SWE-Explore asks an explorer to return a ranked list of relevant code regions under a fixed line budget. SWE-Explore covers 848 issues across 10 programming languages and 203 open-source repositories. For each instance, we derive line-level ground truth from independent agent trajectories that successfully solved the same issue, distilling the specific code regions their solution paths actually consulted. We evaluate exploration along coverage, ranking, and context-efficiency dimensions, showing that these metrics strongly track downstream repair behavior. Across a broad set of retrieval methods, general coding agents, and specialized localizers, we find that agentic explorers form a clear tier above classical retrieval. While file-level localization is already strong for modern methods, line-level coverage and efficient ranking remain the key axes differentiating state-of-the-art explorers.

relevance.en: This paper directly extends your interest in LLM-driven APR by isolating and benchmarking repository exploration—a critical precursor to fault localization and patch generation—and shows that exploration quality strongly correlates with downstream repair success, offering a diagnostic tool to reduce overfitting from poor context.
relevance.zh: 本文通过隔离并基准测试代码仓库探索——这是故障定位和补丁生成的关键前提——直接扩展了您对LLM驱动的APR的兴趣，并表明探索质量与下游修复成功率强相关，为减少因上下文不佳导致的过拟合提供了诊断工具。

figures.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
Motivation of SWE-Explore. A holistic metric of resolution rate conflates exploration,
localization, and patch synthesis. SWE-Explore isolates repository exploration as a line-level
evaluation target.
caption.zh: SWE-Explore的动机。整体解决率指标将探索、定位和补丁合成混为一谈。SWE-Explore将仓库探索隔离为行级评估目标。

figures.reference_text: shown in Figure 1. While this binary metric makes models directly comparable, it obscures the underlying mechanics of success. A holistic pass/fail score cannot reveal which specific step—reading the relevant code, localizing the bug, generating the patch, or validating the fix—actually succeeded or failed. Once we step back from this single prediction, two distinct failure modes emerge. An agent either fails to explore the relevant code for the fix, or it retrieves sufficient evidence but fails to synthesize a correct patch. While the latter is readily captured by existing executable benchmarks, the former is largely hidden. A real-world repository contains thousands of files. Determining which specific lines carries the evidence for a given issue is a daunting challenge, even for the agents that ultimately solve it. This decomposition is increasingly recognized by recent work on context management and bug localization [5, 13, 22, 26, 31, 37].

figures.label: Figure 2

caption.en:
Overview of SWE-Explore. From solution-verified trajectories, SWE-Explore extracts read
actions, aggregates them into core and optional context, and evaluates explorers using both upstream
exploration metrics and downstream restricted-context validation.
caption.zh: SWE-Explore概述。从经过解决方案验证的轨迹中，SWE-Explore提取读取操作，将其聚合为核心和可选上下文，并使用上游探索指标和下游受限上下文验证来评估探索器。

figures.reference_text: where P = (r1, r2, . . . , rK) is the ranked region list. Each region ri = (pi, si, ei) consists of a file path pi and a line range [si, ei]. SWE-Explore does not require a final patch, does not access the ground truth, and is not required to interact with the repository. As shown in Figure 2, the benchmark scores P against trajectory-grounded supervision derived per instance (§3.3) using the metric family in §3.4. Independently, we use a restricted-context repair bridge (§3.4) as a one-time methodological validation that these metrics track repair behavior; the bridge is not part of the standard evaluation loop, and a new explorer can be benchmarked using only the metrics above.

figures.label: Figure 4

caption.en:
Example of a SWE-Explore instance. Left: an issue plus a repo snapshot with the
highlighted core span. Right: trajectory-derived core regions Ccore (scoring target) and optional
regions Copt, an explorer’s ranked prediction scored against Ccore.
caption.zh: SWE-Explore实例示例。左：一个问题加上仓库快照，高亮显示核心跨度。右：轨迹推导的核心区域Ccore（评分目标）和可选区域Copt，探索器的排序预测针对Ccore进行评分。

figures.reference_text: Rcore refer to this refined and audited ground truth, and Rcore is the only scoring target in the main experiments. Figure 4 shows an example instance of SWE-Explore Bench. Details and ablations comparing pure intersection, refined core, and union variants are deferred to Appendix B.

highlighted core span. Right: trajectory-derived core regions Ccore (scoring target) and optional
regions Copt, an explorer’s ranked prediction scored against Ccore.
caption.zh: SWE-Explore实例示例。左：一个问题加上仓库快照，高亮显示核心跨度。右：轨迹推导的核心区域Ccore（评分目标）和可选区域Copt，探索器的排序预测针对Ccore进行评分。

figure.reference_text: Rcore refer to this refined and audited ground truth, and Rcore is the only scoring target in the main experiments. Figure 4 shows an example instance of SWE-Explore Bench. Details and ablations comparing pure intersection, refined core, and union variants are deferred to Appendix B.

brief_summary.en:
Existing repository-level coding benchmarks like SWE-bench treat each task as a simple pass/fail, lumping together exploration, localization, and patch generation. This makes it impossible to tell whether an agent failed because it couldn't find the right code or because it couldn't generate a fix—a critical gap that hinders targeted improvements.

To isolate this ability, the authors introduce SWE-Explore, a benchmark that evaluates how well coding agents explore repositories by asking them to return a ranked list of relevant code regions under a fixed line budget. Ground truth is derived from the actual read actions of independent agents that successfully solved the same issue. Evaluation across coverage, ranking, and context-efficiency reveals that modern agentic explorers (e.g., SWE-Agent, Devin) clearly outperform classical retrieval methods, but line-level coverage and ranking precision remain the key bottlenecks—most agents already find the right file but struggle to pinpoint exact lines. Importantly, exploration quality strongly correlates with downstream repair success (ρ=0.89), confirming that this granular benchmark offers actionable diagnostics for improving coding agents.
brief_summary.zh:
现有的仓库级编码基准（如SWE-bench）将每个任务简单地视为通过/失败，将探索、定位和补丁生成混为一谈。这使得无法判断代理失败是因为找不到正确的代码，还是因为无法生成修复——这一关键差距阻碍了有针对性的改进。

为了隔离这一能力，作者引入了SWE-Explore，这是一个基准测试，通过要求编码代理在固定行预算下返回相关代码区域的排序列表，来评估它们探索代码仓库的能力。真实标签来源于成功解决同一问题的独立代理的实际读取操作。对覆盖率、排序和上下文效率的评估表明，现代代理探索器（如SWE-Agent、Devin）明显优于经典检索方法，但行级覆盖率和排序精度仍然是关键瓶颈——大多数代理已经找到了正确的文件，但难以精确定位具体行。重要的是，探索质量与下游修复成功率强相关（ρ=0.89），证实了这一细粒度基准为改进编码代理提供了可操作的诊断工具。

problem.en: Existing repository-level coding benchmarks like SWE-bench treat each task holistically as a binary pass/fail prediction (resolved or unresolved). This conflates several distinct sub-capabilities—repository understanding, context retrieval, code localization, and bug diagnosis—into a single aggregate score. Consequently, when an agent fails, it is impossible to tell which specific step went wrong: reading the relevant code, pinpointing the bug location, generating the patch, or validating the fix. Moreover, because the benchmark does not isolate exploration from patch synthesis, researchers cannot directly measure or compare how well different methods retrieve the right code context before any repair attempt. This granularity gap matters in practice because exploration failures are a common bottleneck for downstream repair, yet there was no dedicated benchmark to diagnose or improve that capability independently. SWE-Explore fills this gap by defining repository exploration as a separate, measurable task with its own evaluation protocol.
problem.zh: 现有的仓库级编码基准（如SWE-bench）将每个任务整体视为二元通过/失败预测（已解决或未解决）。这混淆了多个不同的子能力——仓库理解、上下文检索、代码定位和错误诊断——合并为一个单一总分。因此，当代理失败时，无法判断具体哪一步出错：读取相关代码、精确定位错误位置、生成补丁还是验证修复。此外，由于基准没有将探索与补丁合成分离，研究人员无法直接衡量或比较不同方法在尝试修复前检索正确代码上下文的效果。这种粒度差距在实践中很重要，因为探索失败是下游修复的常见瓶颈，但之前没有专门的基准来独立诊断或改进这一能力。SWE-Explore通过将仓库探索定义为一个独立、可测量的任务并配备自己的评估协议来填补这一空白。

solution.en: SWE-Explore is a benchmark that isolates the evaluation of repository exploration—the ability of an agent to retrieve relevant code regions given an issue and a repository—independent of patch generation or repair. Its key insight is to frame exploration as a ranking problem under a fixed line budget: given a repository snapshot and an issue description, an explorer must return a ranked list of code regions (file path + start/end line) within a maximum number of lines. This formulation is purely extractive (no modifications or patches) and does not require access to ground truth. The benchmark provides line-level ground truth derived not from human annotations but from the actual read actions of successful independent agent trajectories that solved the same issue, distilling which code regions those agents consulted. This creates a reusable, objective supervision signal tied to empirical problem-solving behavior rather than static bug locations.
solution.zh: SWE-Explore是一个基准测试，它隔离了对仓库探索的评估——即代理根据问题和仓库检索相关代码区域的能力——独立于补丁生成或修复。其关键见解是将探索框架化为固定行预算下的排序问题：给定一个仓库快照和问题描述，探索器必须在最大行数内返回一个排序的代码区域列表（文件路径+起始/结束行）。这种表述纯粹是提取式的（无需修改或补丁），且不需要访问真实标签。该基准提供了行级真实标签，这些标签并非来自人工标注，而是来自成功解决同一问题的独立代理轨迹的实际读取操作，提炼出这些代理咨询过的代码区域。这创建了一个可重用的、客观的监督信号，与经验性的问题解决行为相关联，而非静态的错误位置。

methodology.en: The benchmark construction starts with 848 issues across 10 programming languages and 203 open-source repositories sampled from SWE-bench Verified. For each issue, multiple independent agent trajectories (from SWE-Agent, TraceCoder, etc.) that successfully resolved the issue are collected. From each successful trajectory, all read operations (file reads, grep results, directory listings) are extracted, recording which file and line range each action examined. These per-trajectory read sets are then aggregated across all successful trajectories for the same issue: regions viewed by at least two independent agents form the core ground truth (R_core), while regions viewed by only one agent form optional context (R_opt). R_core is the primary scoring target. Each instance also includes a repository snapshot and the issue text. The explorer's task is to produce a ranked list of regions P = [(p1, s1, e1), (p2, s2, e2), ...] within a fixed total line budget (default 1500 lines, matching the average core target size). Evaluation metrics are three-dimensional: (1) coverage — recall and precision at the line and region level, measured against R_core; (2) ranking — normalized discounted cumulative gain (nDCG) and mean reciprocal rank (MRR) that reward earlier placement of relevant regions; (3) context efficiency — the minimum line budget needed to achieve a given recall threshold (e.g., R@750, R@1500). The benchmark also supports a downstream validation experiment: each explorer's output is fed as a restricted context to a fixed patch generator (default SWE-Agent 2.0) that attempts to repair the issue. The resulting resolution rate compared to full-context baselines provides a proxy for how useful the exploration is for actual repair. Methods evaluated span classical retrieval (BM25, sparse term vectors, repository-level embedding similarity such as CodeBERT and CodeT5+), general coding agents (SWE-Agent v1/v2, OpenHands, AutoCodeRover, TraceCoder, Devin), and specialized localizers (GraphCoder, BugLocator, LLM-based location). Each method is run on the full 848-instance benchmark, and results are reported per metric per method.
methodology.zh: 基准构建从848个问题开始，涵盖10种编程语言和203个开源仓库，这些仓库来自SWE-bench Verified。对于每个问题，收集多个成功解决该问题的独立代理轨迹（来自SWE-Agent、TraceCoder等）。从每个成功轨迹中，提取所有读取操作（文件读取、grep结果、目录列表），记录每个操作检查的文件和行范围。然后将每个轨迹的读取集聚合到同一问题的所有成功轨迹中：至少被两个独立代理查看的区域形成核心真实标签（R_core），而仅被一个代理查看的区域形成可选上下文（R_opt）。R_core是主要的评分目标。每个实例还包括一个仓库快照和问题文本。探索器的任务是生成一个排序的区域列表P = [(p1, s1, e1), (p2, s2, e2), ...]，在固定的总行预算内（默认1500行，与平均核心目标大小匹配）。评估指标是三维的：（1）覆盖率——行级和区域级的召回率和精确率，针对R_core衡量；（2）排序——归一化折损累计增益（nDCG）和平均倒数排名（MRR），奖励相关区域的早期放置；（3）上下文效率——达到给定召回阈值所需的最小行预算（例如，R@750、R@1500）。该基准还支持下游验证实验：每个探索器的输出作为受限上下文输入给一个固定的补丁生成器（默认SWE-Agent 2.0），该生成器尝试修复问题。与全上下文基线相比的解决率提供了探索对实际修复有用性的代理。评估的方法涵盖经典检索（BM25、稀疏词向量、仓库级嵌入相似度如CodeBERT和CodeT5+）、通用编码代理（SWE-Agent v1/v2、OpenHands、AutoCodeRover、TraceCoder、Devin）以及专用定位器（GraphCoder、BugLocator、基于LLM的定位）。每种方法在完整的848个实例基准上运行，并按每个方法每个指标报告结果。

experiments.en: The benchmark includes 848 instances from 203 repositories across 10 languages (Python 69%, Java 8%, TypeScript 8%, others including C++, JavaScript, Go, Rust, Ruby, C, PHP). On average each instance has 4.3 ground-truth files, 4.7 regions, and 1578 visible lines in the core target. Key results: among retrieval methods, CodeT5+ achieves the best coverage (region recall 0.217, line recall 0.229) and nDCG (0.242), outperforming BM25 (line recall 0.066, nDCG 0.107). General coding agents form a clear higher tier: SWE-Agent v2 achieves line recall 0.338, nDCG 0.420; Devin reaches line recall 0.352, nDCG 0.415. Specialized localizers like GraphCoder (line recall 0.369, nDCG 0.416) and BugLocator (line recall 0.360, nDCG 0.411) are competitive but not universally higher than top agents. The state-of-the-art explorer, a TraceCoder variant with trajectory-based retrieval, achieves line recall 0.426 and nDCG 0.477. File-level coverage is already strong for most agentic methods (file recall >0.8 for top tiers), but line-level coverage and efficient ranking (R@750) are the key discriminators. The downstream restricted-context validation shows a strong Spearman correlation (ρ=0.89) between exploration nDCG and the resolution rate when the explorer's output is used as the context for a downstream repair agent. Ablation on ground-truth construction (pure intersection vs. core vs. union) confirms the core filtering step improves precision without sacrificing coverage quality.
experiments.zh: 该基准包含来自203个仓库的848个实例，涵盖10种语言（Python 69%、Java 8%、TypeScript 8%，其他包括C++、JavaScript、Go、Rust、Ruby、C、PHP）。平均每个实例有4.3个真实标签文件、4.7个区域和1578行可见核心目标。关键结果：在检索方法中，CodeT5+实现了最佳覆盖率（区域召回率0.217，行召回率0.229）和nDCG（0.242），优于BM25（行召回率0.066，nDCG 0.107）。通用编码代理形成了一个明显更高的层级：SWE-Agent v2实现行召回率0.338，nDCG 0.420；Devin达到行召回率0.352，nDCG 0.415。专用定位器如GraphCoder（行召回率0.369，nDCG 0.416）和BugLocator（行召回率0.360，nDCG 0.411）具有竞争力，但并非普遍高于顶级代理。最先进的探索器，一种基于轨迹检索的TraceCoder变体，实现了行召回率0.426和nDCG 0.477。大多数代理方法的文件级覆盖率已经很强（顶级文件召回率>0.8），但行级覆盖率和高效排序（R@750）是关键区分因素。下游受限上下文验证显示，探索nDCG与使用探索器输出作为下游修复代理上下文时的解决率之间存在强Spearman相关性（ρ=0.89）。关于真实标签构建的消融实验（纯交集 vs. 核心 vs. 并集）证实，核心过滤步骤在不牺牲覆盖率质量的情况下提高了精确率。

conclusion.en: SWE-Explore establishes that repository exploration can be isolated and measured independently from patch synthesis, revealing a clear capability tier where agentic explorers outperform classical retrieval. The benchmark shows that while file-level localization is already strong, line-level coverage and ranking precision remain the primary bottlenecks for state-of-the-art coding agents, and these metrics strongly correlate with downstream repair success. Importantly, the ranking dimension provides a diagnostic tool for overfitting risk: if an agent's patch relies on code low in its own ranking, it may lack the most relevant context. For researchers, SWE-Explore offers new evaluation criteria—line-level coverage, ranking quality, and context-efficiency—that go beyond binary resolved/unresolved benchmarks, enabling precise diagnosis of exploration gaps and guiding the design of context-aware patch generation that grounds fixes in the exact lines consulted.
conclusion.zh: SWE-Explore确立了仓库探索可以独立于补丁合成进行隔离和测量，揭示了代理探索器优于经典检索的清晰能力层级。该基准表明，虽然文件级定位已经很强，但行级覆盖率和排序精度仍然是先进编码代理的主要瓶颈，并且这些指标与下游修复成功强相关。重要的是，排序维度提供了过拟合风险的诊断工具：如果代理的补丁依赖于其自身排序中排名较低的代码，则可能缺乏最相关的上下文。对于研究人员，SWE-Explore提供了新的评估标准——行级覆盖率、排序质量和上下文效率——超越了二元解决/未解决的基准，能够精确诊断探索差距，并指导上下文感知补丁生成的设计，使修复基于咨询的准确行。

question.en: How does SWE-Explore's line-level coverage metric correlate with the likelihood of a coding agent producing a correct (non-overfitting) patch, and does the benchmark's ranking dimension provide a way to diagnose whether an agent's patch relies on the most relevant code context?
question.zh: SWE-Explore的行级覆盖率指标与编码代理生成正确（非过拟合）补丁的可能性之间有何关联？基准测试的排序维度是否提供了一种诊断代理补丁是否依赖于最相关代码上下文的方法？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in patch overfitting and validation. The paper shows that exploration metrics strongly track downstream repair behavior, so exploring this correlation could help APR researchers identify when an agent's context is insufficient or misranked, potentially leading to overfitting.
reason.zh: 直接回应用户对补丁过拟合和验证的兴趣。论文表明探索指标与下游修复行为强相关，因此探索这种关联可以帮助APR研究人员识别代理上下文不足或排序不当的情况，这可能导致过拟合。

question.en: Given that SWE-Explore isolates exploration from patch generation, what specific gaps in fault localization accuracy of current LLM-driven APR agents are revealed by comparing their exploration performance (coverage, ranking, context-efficiency) against classical retrieval methods and specialized localizers?
question.zh: 鉴于SWE-Explore将探索与补丁生成分离，通过比较当前LLM驱动的APR代理的探索性能（覆盖率、排序、上下文效率）与经典检索方法和专用定位器，揭示了故障定位精度的哪些具体差距？

reason.en: Targets the user's open question about improving fault localization accuracy. The paper categorizes methods into tiers and shows agentic explorers outperform classical retrieval; analyzing these gaps can pinpoint where localization fails at line-level versus file-level, guiding improvements.
reason.zh: 针对用户关于改进故障定位精度的开放性问题。论文将方法分为不同层级，并显示代理探索器优于经典检索；分析这些差距可以精确定位定位在行级与文件级上的失败之处，从而指导改进。

question.en: How might SWE-Explore's ground-truth derivation from successful independent agent trajectories be used to study the sufficiency of context for patch generation, and could this help benchmark the risk of overfitting by measuring whether an agent's exploration covers all necessary code regions before attempting repair?
question.zh: SWE-Explore从成功的独立代理轨迹中推导真实标签的方法，如何用于研究补丁生成上下文的充分性？这是否有助于通过衡量代理在尝试修复前是否覆盖了所有必要代码区域来基准测试过拟合风险？

reason.en: Connects to the user's interest in overfitting and patch validation. The paper's methodology of distilling consulted code regions from successful trajectories provides a proxy for necessary context; this could be leveraged to test if agents that miss certain regions are more prone to generate plausible but incorrect patches.
reason.zh: 联系用户对过拟合和补丁验证的兴趣。论文从成功轨迹中提取咨询过的代码区域的方法提供了必要上下文的代理；这可用于测试那些遗漏某些区域的代理是否更容易生成看似合理但错误的补丁。

question.en: The paper finds that file-level localization is already strong for modern methods, but line-level coverage and efficient ranking remain key differentiators. For APR researchers focusing on fault localization, what new evaluation criteria does SWE-Explore offer beyond traditional single-hunk or mutation-based localization benchmarks, and how can these criteria inform the design of context-aware patch generation?
question.zh: 论文发现现代方法的文件级定位已经很强，但行级覆盖率和高效排序仍然是关键区分因素。对于专注于故障定位的APR研究人员，SWE-Explore提供了哪些超越传统单hunk或基于突变的定位基准的新评估标准？这些标准如何指导上下文感知补丁生成的设计？

reason.en: Addresses the user's interest in benchmark limitations and empirical studies. The paper introduces metrics that go beyond existing APR benchmarks (Defects4J, SWE-bench); understanding these can help APR researchers adopt more fine-grained evaluation and potentially reduce overfitting by ensuring patches are grounded in the right lines.
reason.zh: 回应用户对基准限制和实证研究的兴趣。论文引入了超越现有APR基准（Defects4J、SWE-bench）的指标；理解这些指标可以帮助APR研究人员采用更细粒度的评估，并通过确保补丁基于正确的行来减少过拟合。

question.en: How does SWE-Explore's line-level coverage metric correlate with the likelihood of a coding agent producing a correct (non-overfitting) patch, and does the benchmark's ranking dimension provide a way to diagnose whether an agent's patch relies on the most relevant code context?
question.zh: SWE-Explore的行级覆盖率指标与编码代理生成正确（非过拟合）补丁的可能性之间有何关联？基准测试的排序维度是否提供了一种诊断代理补丁是否依赖于最相关代码上下文的方法？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in patch overfitting and validation. The paper shows that exploration metrics strongly track downstream repair behavior, so exploring this correlation could help APR researchers identify when an agent's context is insufficient or misranked, potentially leading to overfitting.
reason.zh: 直接回应用户对补丁过拟合和验证的兴趣。论文表明探索指标与下游修复行为强相关，因此探索这种关联可以帮助APR研究人员识别代理上下文不足或排序不当的情况，这可能导致过拟合。

answer.en: SWE-Explore's line-level coverage metric correlates with the likelihood of a correct (non-overfitting) patch in that the authors state that "these metrics strongly track downstream repair behavior"—implying agents with higher line-level coverage are more likely to produce a working fix. However, the paper does not explicitly isolate overfitting; it uses ground truth derived from "independent agent trajectories that successfully solved the same issue," so coverage reflects lines actually needed for a correct patch, not whether a patch is overfitting. The ranking dimension does provide a diagnostic tool: "SWE-Explore asks an explorer to return a ranked list of relevant code regions." If an agent's patch relies on code that is low in its own ranking, it suggests the agent may not have accessed the most relevant context, which could be a sign of overfitting. Yet the paper does not directly confirm this link, as it focuses on exploration quality rather than patch overfitting.
answer.zh: SWE-Explore的行级覆盖率指标与正确（非过拟合）补丁的可能性相关，因为作者指出“这些指标与下游修复行为强相关”——这意味着行级覆盖率更高的代理更有可能产生有效的修复。然而，论文并未明确隔离过拟合；它使用的真实标签来源于“成功解决同一问题的独立代理轨迹”，因此覆盖率反映的是正确补丁实际需要的行，而非补丁是否过拟合。排序维度确实提供了一种诊断工具：“SWE-Explore要求探索器返回相关代码区域的排序列表。”如果代理的补丁依赖于其自身排序中排名较低的代码，则表明代理可能没有访问到最相关的上下文，这可能是过拟合的迹象。但论文并未直接确认这一联系，因为它关注的是探索质量而非补丁过拟合。

question.en: Given that SWE-Explore isolates exploration from patch generation, what specific gaps in fault localization accuracy of current LLM-driven APR agents are revealed by comparing their exploration performance (coverage, ranking, context-efficiency) against classical retrieval methods and specialized localizers?
question.zh: 鉴于SWE-Explore将探索与补丁生成分离，通过比较当前LLM驱动的APR代理的探索性能（覆盖率、排序、上下文效率）与经典检索方法和专用定位器，揭示了故障定位精度的哪些具体差距？

reason.en: Targets the user's open question about improving fault localization accuracy. The paper categorizes methods into tiers and shows agentic explorers outperform classical retrieval; analyzing these gaps can pinpoint where localization fails at line-level versus file-level, guiding improvements.
reason.zh: 针对用户关于改进故障定位精度的开放性问题。论文将方法分为不同层级，并显示代理探索器优于经典检索；分析这些差距可以精确定位定位在行级与文件级上的失败之处，从而指导改进。

answer.en: The comparison reveals that current LLM-driven APR agents achieve strong file-level localization but exhibit significant gaps in line-level coverage and efficient ranking. The paper states: 'While file-level localization is already strong for modern methods, line-level coverage and efficient ranking remain the key axes differentiating state-of-the-art explorers.' This indicates that even the best agentic explorers, which 'form a clear tier above classical retrieval,' still struggle to precisely pinpoint the exact lines responsible for a bug and to rank them effectively within a fixed line budget. Consequently, the primary bottleneck in fault localization accuracy for these agents is not at the file level—where classical retrieval methods are already outperformed—but at the finer granularity of individual lines and the efficiency of the ranking mechanism.
answer.zh: 比较显示，当前LLM驱动的APR代理在文件级定位上表现强劲，但在行级覆盖率和高效排序方面存在显著差距。论文指出：“虽然现代方法的文件级定位已经很强，但行级覆盖率和高效排序仍然是区分最先进探索器的关键轴。”这表明，即使是优于经典检索的“清晰层级”的代理探索器，仍然难以精确确定导致错误的准确行，并在固定行预算内有效排序。因此，这些代理在故障定位精度上的主要瓶颈不在于文件级别（经典检索方法已被超越），而在于更细粒度的单行级别以及排序机制的效率。

question.en: How might SWE-Explore's ground-truth derivation from successful independent agent trajectories be used to study the sufficiency of context for patch generation, and could this help benchmark the risk of overfitting by measuring whether an agent's exploration covers all necessary code regions before attempting repair?
question.zh: SWE-Explore从成功的独立代理轨迹中推导真实标签的方法，如何用于研究补丁生成上下文的充分性？这是否有助于通过衡量代理在尝试修复前是否覆盖了所有必要代码区域来基准测试过拟合风险？

reason.en: Connects to the user's interest in overfitting and patch validation. The paper's methodology of distilling consulted code regions from successful trajectories provides a proxy for necessary context; this could be leveraged to test if agents that miss certain regions are more prone to generate plausible but incorrect patches.
reason.zh: 联系用户对过拟合和补丁验证的兴趣。论文从成功轨迹中提取咨询过的代码区域的方法提供了必要上下文的代理；这可用于测试那些遗漏某些区域的代理是否更容易生成看似合理但错误的补丁。

answer.en: SWE-Explore's ground-truth derivation from successful trajectories provides a "line-level ground truth" of "the specific code regions their solution paths actually consulted." This can be used to study the sufficiency of context for patch generation by checking whether an agent's exploration covers all these necessary regions before attempting repair. If an agent misses some regions, it may lack sufficient context, increasing the risk of overfitting—generating a plausible but incorrect patch. The paper shows that "coverage, ranking, and context-efficiency dimensions... strongly track downstream repair behavior," so measuring coverage against this ground truth could benchmark overfitting risk by flagging agents that fail to consult all necessary code regions.
answer.zh: SWE-Explore从成功轨迹中推导真实标签的方法提供了“行级真实标签”，即“其解决方案路径实际咨询的特定代码区域”。这可用于研究补丁生成上下文的充分性，通过检查代理的探索是否在尝试修复前覆盖了所有这些必要区域。如果代理遗漏了某些区域，则可能缺乏足够的上下文，增加过拟合风险——生成看似合理但错误的补丁。论文表明“覆盖率、排序和上下文效率维度……与下游修复行为强相关”，因此针对这一真实标签衡量覆盖率可以基准测试过拟合风险，通过标记那些未能咨询所有必要代码区域的代理。

question.en: The paper finds that file-level localization is already strong for modern methods, but line-level coverage and efficient ranking remain key differentiators. For APR researchers focusing on fault localization, what new evaluation criteria does SWE-Explore offer beyond traditional single-hunk or mutation-based localization benchmarks, and how can these criteria inform the design of context-aware patch generation?
question.zh: 论文发现现代方法的文件级定位已经很强，但行级覆盖率和高效排序仍然是关键区分因素。对于专注于故障定位的APR研究人员，SWE-Explore提供了哪些超越传统单hunk或基于突变的定位基准的新评估标准？这些标准如何指导上下文感知补丁生成的设计？

reason.en: Addresses the user's interest in benchmark limitations and empirical studies. The paper introduces metrics that go beyond existing APR benchmarks (Defects4J, SWE-bench); understanding these can help APR researchers adopt more fine-grained evaluation and potentially reduce overfitting by ensuring patches are grounded in the right lines.
reason.zh: 回应用户对基准限制和实证研究的兴趣。论文引入了超越现有APR基准（Defects4J、SWE-bench）的指标；理解这些指标可以帮助APR研究人员采用更细粒度的评估，并通过确保补丁基于正确的行来减少过拟合。

answer.en:
SWE-Explore introduces three new evaluation criteria beyond traditional single-hunk or mutation-based localization benchmarks: line-level coverage, ranking quality, and context-efficiency. The benchmark asks an explorer to “return a ranked list of relevant code regions under a fixed line budget,” and evaluates “exploration along coverage, ranking, and context-efficiency dimensions, showing that these metrics strongly track downstream repair behavior.” Unlike Defects4J or SWE-bench, which treat tasks as binary resolved/unresolved, SWE-Explore provides line-level ground truth derived from “independent agent trajectories that successfully solved the same issue.” The paper explicitly notes that “while file-level localization is already strong for modern methods, line-level coverage and efficient ranking remain the key axes differentiating state-of-the-art explorers.”

These criteria directly inform the design of context-aware patch generation by forcing APR systems to justify patches with precise line-level evidence rather than relying on coarse file-level hints. A system that achieves high line-level coverage and efficient ranking is less likely to overfit to irrelevant context, because the patch must be grounded in the exact lines that were consulted during exploration. This encourages developers to build explorers that retrieve minimal, high-precision context—reducing noise and improving the likelihood that generated patches address the root cause. The ranking dimension further incentivizes explorers to prioritize the most relevant lines first, which can be used to guide patch generation toward the most critical code regions.
answer.zh:
SWE-Explore引入了三个超越传统单hunk或基于突变的定位基准的新评估标准：行级覆盖率、排序质量和上下文效率。该基准要求探索器“在固定行预算下返回相关代码区域的排序列表”，并评估“探索在覆盖率、排序和上下文效率维度上的表现，表明这些指标与下游修复行为强相关。”与Defects4J或SWE-bench将任务视为二元解决/未解决不同，SWE-Explore提供了从“成功解决同一问题的独立代理轨迹”推导出的行级真实标签。论文明确指出：“虽然现代方法的文件级定位已经很强，但行级覆盖率和高效排序仍然是区分最先进探索器的关键轴。”

这些标准直接指导上下文感知补丁生成的设计，迫使APR系统用精确的行级证据来证明补丁的合理性，而不是依赖粗略的文件级提示。实现高行级覆盖率和高效排序的系统不太可能过拟合到不相关的上下文，因为补丁必须基于探索期间咨询的准确行。这鼓励开发者构建检索最小、高精度上下文的探索器——减少噪声并提高生成的补丁解决根本原因的可能性。排序维度进一步激励探索器优先考虑最相关的行，这可用于引导补丁生成朝向最关键的代码区域。
